Why is this medication prescribed? 
Vamorolone is used to treat Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD; a progressive disease in which the muscles do not function properly) in adults and children 2 years of age and older. Vamorolone is in a class of medications called corticosteroids. It works by reducing inflammation (swelling) and by changing the way the immune system works.
How should this medicine be used? 
Vamorolone comes as a suspension (liquid) to take by mouth. It is usually taken once a day with a meal. Take vamorolone at around the same time every day. Follow the directions on your prescription label carefully, and ask your doctor or pharmacist to explain any part you do not understand. Take vamorolone exactly as directed. Do not take more or less of it or take it more often than prescribed by your doctor.
Shake the suspension well for about 30 seconds before each use to mix the medication evenly. The dose of vamorolone should only be measured with the oral syringe that comes with it.
Your doctor may decrease your dose of vamorolone depending on how well the medication works for you. Your doctor may also need to change your dose if you experience unusual stress
